raw_output.summary: Security review of the oksskolten TypeScript/React codebase identified five findings across XSS, token security, and tabnapping vectors. The most actionable issues are: (1) DOMPurify is not configured to rewrite target=_blank links in article content, enabling tabnapping; (2) the auth bearer token lives in localStorage, where any XSS can exfiltrate it; (3) the SanitizedHTML component's sanitization contract is documentation-only with no type or runtime enforcement; (4) streaming AI/translation HTML is rendered live and may bypass the sanitizeHtml step that is only confirmed on the final result; and (5) API tokens copied to clipboard are never cleared. The overall sanitization architecture (DOMPurify + SanitizedHTML) is sound in principle, but the gaps above introduce realistic attack paths via untrusted article and AI content.

findings.title: No rel=noopener noreferrer enforced on sanitized article links — tabnapping risk
findings.description: The DOMPurify configuration in sanitize.ts adds an afterSanitizeAttributes hook only for IMG tags (to inject loading=lazy). It never walks anchor tags to enforce rel=noopener noreferrer on elements with target=_blank. DOMPurify's default allowlist permits both the target and rel attributes on anchor tags, so malicious article HTML containing <a href=https://attacker.com target=_blank> survives sanitization intact. The opened page can set window.opener.location to redirect the parent reader tab (tabnapping). While Chromium 88+ applies implicit noopener, Firefox and older browsers remain vulnerable, and server-sourced RSS/article content is an untrusted input that commonly includes target=_blank links.
findings.recommendation: Extend the existing afterSanitizeAttributes hook to also cover anchor tags: check if node.tagName === 'A' and node.getAttribute('target') === '_blank', then call node.setAttribute('rel', 'noopener noreferrer'). This is a one-liner addition to the existing hook and has zero functional impact on legitimate links.

findings.title: Auth bearer token stored in localStorage — exfiltrable via any XSS
findings.description: src/lib/auth.ts persists the session token under the key auth_token in localStorage and caches it in the module-level variable _token. Any successful XSS injection — via article content that escapes sanitization, a compromised npm dependency, or a malicious browser extension — can call localStorage.getItem('auth_token') and exfiltrate the token to an attacker-controlled server, resulting in full account takeover with no indication to the user. The token is long-lived and is included in every API request via authHeaders() in api-base.ts.
findings.recommendation: Prefer storing the session credential in an HttpOnly cookie set by the server, which is inaccessible to JavaScript entirely. If a bearer-token SPA flow must be retained, use short-lived tokens with refresh-token rotation and sessionStorage (cleared on tab close) to reduce the persistence window. Pair with a strict server-side Content-Security-Policy to reduce XSS exploitability.

findings.title: SanitizedHTML component has documentation-only sanitization contract — no type or runtime enforcement
findings.description: src/components/ui/sanitized-html.tsx renders arbitrary strings via dangerouslySetInnerHTML and relies entirely on caller discipline. The component comment says the caller is responsible for sanitizing, but there is no branded type, runtime assertion, or wrapper that enforces this. Any new call site can pass raw server HTML or AI-generated text and silently introduce XSS. The streamingHtml value returned by useStreamingAI is passed directly to SanitizedHTML in ArticleContentBody; if renderMarkdown output is not passed through sanitizeHtml inside useStreamingAI itself, this is a live XSS vector in the article summary and translation flows.
findings.recommendation: Introduce a branded opaque type SafeHtml (e.g., type SafeHtml = string & { __brand: 'SafeHtml' }) that can only be produced by sanitizeHtml(). Change SanitizedHTML's html prop to accept SafeHtml instead of string. This makes the contract compiler-enforced and immediately surfaces any call site that passes raw strings. As a quick interim step, add a DEV-only runtime check inside the component for obvious patterns like <script or javascript: to catch violations during development.

findings.title: Streaming AI and translation HTML may render unsanitized intermediate content
findings.description: useStreamingAI exposes a streamingHtml value that is rendered live during streaming via SanitizedHTML in ArticleContentBody and ArticleSummarySection. In useSummarize, the final summaryHtml is explicitly sanitized (renderMarkdown then sanitizeHtml), but the in-flight streamingHtml is computed inside useStreamingAI and the available code shows only markdown processing with no evidence of a sanitizeHtml call within the hook. If the streaming path calls renderMarkdown without sanitizing, any HTML injected via prompt-injection in article content forwarded to the LLM would execute in the browser before the final sanitized copy replaces it. The same pattern exists in useTranslate.
findings.recommendation: Ensure sanitizeHtml() is called on the output of renderMarkdown inside useStreamingAI before storing it in streamingHtml, applied on every streaming chunk update. DOMPurify is synchronous and fast enough for per-chunk sanitization. Audit useTranslate for the same pattern to confirm parity with useSummarize.

findings.title: API tokens copied to clipboard without expiry timeout or user warning
findings.description: ApiTokenSettings.handleCopy and GitHubOAuthSettings.handleCopy copy sensitive API tokens and OAuth secrets to the system clipboard. Clipboard contents persist across application focus changes and are accessible to background clipboard managers, browser extensions, and other browser tabs that have clipboard permission. There is no timeout that clears the clipboard after a short interval and no visible warning informing the user that a secret is now in their clipboard.
findings.recommendation: After copying, schedule a setTimeout of approximately 30 seconds to overwrite the clipboard with an empty string via navigator.clipboard.writeText(''). Display a UI hint such as 'Token copied — will clear from clipboard in 30s' so the user is aware of the exposure window. This aligns with OWASP clipboard handling guidance.
